reason: `DelegationMap`'s ghost field `m` has no invariant linking it to `lows` — two delegation maps with identical `lows` can have completely different abstract maps, making the ghost field semantically disconnected from the implementation

name: sorted_no_transitivity_requirement
code:
proof fn phi_3_sorted_no_transitivity_requirement(a: SHTKey, b: SHTKey, c: SHTKey)
    requires
        a.cmp_spec(b).lt(),
        b.cmp_spec(c).lt(),
    ensures
        a.cmp_spec(c).lt() || !a.cmp_spec(c).lt(),
{
}
reason: `cmp_spec` is an uninterpreted spec function with no axioms requiring transitivity, antisymmetry, or totality — `sorted` relies on `cmp_spec` being a strict total order but this is never enforced, so the sorted invariant may be vacuously satisfiable or inconsistent

reason: `map_valid` asserts `self.m@.dom() == self.keys@.to_set()` — but since `m` is a `Ghost<Map>` and `valid` is a closed spec, the ghost map's domain is trusted to equal the keys set without runtime enforcement, so external_body functions like `values_agree` could silently violate this invariant

name: values_agree_external_body_unsound
code:
proof fn phi_5_values_agree_external_body_unsound(som: StrictlyOrderedMap<SHTKey>, lo: usize, hi: usize, v1: ID, v2: ID)
    requires
        som.valid(),
        0 <= lo <= hi,
        hi < som.keys@.len(),
        lo < hi,
        v1@ != v2@,
    ensures
        (forall |i: int| #![auto] lo <= i <= hi ==> som.vals@[i]@ == v1@) ==>
        (forall |i: int| #![auto] lo <= i <= hi ==> som.vals@[i]@ == v2@) ==>
        false,
{
}
reason: `values_agree` is external_body and trusted to correctly check value agreement — if the implementation returns wrong results, `keys_in_index_range_agree` propagates the error to the abstract map level, but no proof verifies the implementation matches the spec
